location: Before Macbeth's castle.  Hautboys and torches
scene_summary: Duncan arrives at Macbeth’s castle, unaware of the planned betrayal.

speaker: INVERNESS
text: Court of Macbeth's castle.
scene_summary: Macbeth sees a visionary dagger and moves toward Duncan’s chamber.

speaker: BANQUO
text: How goes the night, boy?
scene_summary: Macbeth sees a visionary dagger and moves toward Duncan’s chamber.

speaker: FLEANCE
text: The moon is down; I have not heard the clock.
scene_summary: Macbeth sees a visionary dagger and moves toward Duncan’s chamber.

speaker: BANQUO
text: And she goes down at twelve.
scene_summary: Macbeth sees a visionary dagger and moves toward Duncan’s chamber.

speaker: FLEANCE
text: I take't 'tis later, sir.
scene_summary: Macbeth sees a visionary dagger and moves toward Duncan’s chamber.

speaker: BANQUO
text: Hold, take my sword. There's husbandry in heaven, Their candles are all out. Take thee that too. A heavy summons lies like lead upon me, And yet I would not sleep. Merciful powers, Restrain in me the cursed thoughts that nature Gives way to in repose!
scene_summary: Macbeth sees a visionary dagger and moves toward Duncan’s chamber.

speaker: MACBETH
text: A friend.
scene_summary: Macbeth sees a visionary dagger and moves toward Duncan’s chamber.

speaker: BANQUO
text: What, sir, not yet at rest? The King's abed. He hath been in unusual pleasure and Sent forth great largess to your offices. This diamond he greets your wife withal, By the name of most kind hostess, and shut up In measureless content.
scene_summary: Macbeth sees a visionary dagger and moves toward Duncan’s chamber.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Being unprepared, Our will became the servant to defect, Which else should free have wrought.
scene_summary: Macbeth sees a visionary dagger and moves toward Duncan’s chamber.

speaker: BANQUO
text: All's well. I dreamt last night of the three weird sisters: To you they have show'd some truth.
scene_summary: Macbeth sees a visionary dagger and moves toward Duncan’s chamber.

speaker: MACBETH
text: I think not of them; Yet, when we can entreat an hour to serve, We would spend it in some words upon that business, If you would grant the time.
scene_summary: Macbeth sees a visionary dagger and moves toward Duncan’s chamber.

speaker: BANQUO
text: At your kind'st leisure.
scene_summary: Macbeth sees a visionary dagger and moves toward Duncan’s chamber.

speaker: MACBETH
text: If you shall cleave to my consent, when 'tis, It shall make honor for you.
scene_summary: Macbeth sees a visionary dagger and moves toward Duncan’s chamber.

speaker: BANQUO
text: So I lose none In seeking to augment it, but still keep My bosom franchised and allegiance clear, I shall be counsel'd.
scene_summary: Macbeth sees a visionary dagger and moves toward Duncan’s chamber.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Good repose the while.
scene_summary: Macbeth sees a visionary dagger and moves toward Duncan’s chamber.

speaker: BANQUO
text: Thanks, sir, the like to you.
scene_summary: Macbeth sees a visionary dagger and moves toward Duncan’s chamber.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Go bid thy mistress, when my drink is ready, She strike upon the bell. Get thee to bed.     Exit Servant. Is this a dagger which I see before me, The handle toward my hand? Come, let me clutch thee. I have thee not, and yet I see thee still. Art thou not, fatal vision, sensible To feeling as to sight? Or art thou but A dagger of the mind, a false creation, Proceeding from the heat-oppressed brain? I see thee yet, in form as palpable As this which now I draw. Thou marshal'st me the way that I was going, And such an instrument I was to use. Mine eyes are made the fools o' the other senses, Or else worth all the rest. I see thee still, And on thy blade and dudgeon gouts of blood, Which was not so before. There's no such thing: It is the bloody business which informs Thus to mine eyes. Now o'er the one half-world Nature seems dead, and wicked dreams abuse The curtain'd sleep; witchcraft celebrates Pale Hecate's offerings; and wither'd Murther, Alarum'd by his sentinel, the wolf, Whose howl's his watch, thus with his stealthy pace, With Tarquin's ravishing strides, towards his design Moves like a ghost. Thou sure and firm-set earth, Hear not my steps, which way they walk, for fear Thy very stones prate of my whereabout, And take the present horror from the time, Which now suits with it. Whiles I threat, he lives; Words to the heat of deeds too cold breath gives. A bell rings. I go, and it is done; the bell invites me. Hear it not, Duncan, for it is a knell That summons thee to heaven, or to hell.               Exit.
scene_summary: Macbeth sees a visionary dagger and moves toward Duncan’s chamber.

speaker: LADY MACBETH
text: That which hath made them drunk hath made me bold; What hath quench'd them hath given me fire. Hark! Peace! It was the owl that shriek'd, the fatal bellman, Which gives the stern'st good night. He is about it: The doors are open, and the surfeited grooms Do mock their charge with snores. I have drugg'd their possets That death and nature do contend about them, Whether they live or die.
scene_summary: Macbeth murders Duncan, and the couple reacts to guilt and fear.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Who's there? what, ho!
scene_summary: Macbeth murders Duncan, and the couple reacts to guilt and fear.

speaker: LADY MACBETH
text: Alack, I am afraid they have awaked And 'tis not done. The attempt and not the deed Confounds us. Hark! I laid their daggers ready; He could not miss 'em. Had he not resembled My father as he slept, I had done't.
scene_summary: Macbeth murders Duncan, and the couple reacts to guilt and fear.

speaker: MACBETH
text: I have done the deed. Didst thou not hear a noise?
scene_summary: Macbeth murders Duncan, and the couple reacts to guilt and fear.

speaker: LADY MACBETH
text: I heard the owl scream and the crickets cry. Did not you speak?
scene_summary: Macbeth murders Duncan, and the couple reacts to guilt and fear.

speaker: MACBETH
text: When?
scene_summary: Macbeth murders Duncan, and the couple reacts to guilt and fear.

speaker: LADY MACBETH
text: Now.
scene_summary: Macbeth murders Duncan, and the couple reacts to guilt and fear.

speaker: MACBETH
text: As I descended?
scene_summary: Macbeth murders Duncan, and the couple reacts to guilt and fear.

speaker: LADY MACBETH
text: Ay.
scene_summary: Macbeth murders Duncan, and the couple reacts to guilt and fear.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Hark! Who lies i' the second chamber?
scene_summary: Macbeth murders Duncan, and the couple reacts to guilt and fear.

speaker: LADY MACBETH
text: Donalbain.
scene_summary: Macbeth murders Duncan, and the couple reacts to guilt and fear.

speaker: MACBETH
text: This is a sorry sight.           Looks on his hands.
scene_summary: Macbeth murders Duncan, and the couple reacts to guilt and fear.

speaker: LADY MACBETH
text: A foolish thought, to say a sorry sight.
scene_summary: Macbeth murders Duncan, and the couple reacts to guilt and fear.

speaker: MACBETH
text: There's one did laugh in 's sleep, and one cried, "Murther!" That they did wake each other. I stood and heard them, But they did say their prayers and address'd them Again to sleep.
scene_summary: Macbeth murders Duncan, and the couple reacts to guilt and fear.

speaker: LADY MACBETH
text: There are two lodged together.
scene_summary: Macbeth murders Duncan, and the couple reacts to guilt and fear.

speaker: MACBETH
text: One cried, "God bless us!" and "Amen" the other, As they had seen me with these hangman's hands. Listening their fear, I could not say "Amen," When they did say, "God bless us!"
scene_summary: Macbeth murders Duncan, and the couple reacts to guilt and fear.

speaker: LADY MACBETH
text: Consider it not so deeply.
scene_summary: Macbeth murders Duncan, and the couple reacts to guilt and fear.

speaker: MACBETH
text: But wherefore could not I pronounce "Amen"? I had most need of blessing, and "Amen" Stuck in my throat.
scene_summary: Macbeth murders Duncan, and the couple reacts to guilt and fear.

speaker: LADY MACBETH
text: These deeds must not be thought After these ways; so, it will make us mad.
scene_summary: Macbeth murders Duncan, and the couple reacts to guilt and fear.

speaker: MACBETH
text: I heard a voice cry, "Sleep no more! Macbeth does murther sleep" -the innocent sleep, Sleep that knits up the ravel'd sleave of care, The death of each day's life, sore labor's bath, Balm of hurt minds, great nature's second course, Chief nourisher in life's feast-
scene_summary: Macbeth murders Duncan, and the couple reacts to guilt and fear.

speaker: LADY MACBETH
text: What do you mean?
scene_summary: Macbeth murders Duncan, and the couple reacts to guilt and fear.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Still it cried, "Sleep no more!" to all the house; "Glamis hath murther'd sleep, and therefore Cawdor Shall sleep no more. Macbeth shall sleep no more."
scene_summary: Macbeth murders Duncan, and the couple reacts to guilt and fear.

speaker: LADY MACBETH
text: Who was it that thus cried? Why, worthy Thane, You do unbend your noble strength, to think So brainsickly of things. Go, get some water And wash this filthy witness from your hand. Why did you bring these daggers from the place? They must lie there. Go carry them, and smear The sleepy grooms with blood.
scene_summary: Macbeth murders Duncan, and the couple reacts to guilt and fear.

speaker: MACBETH
text: I'll go no more. I am afraid to think what I have done; Look on't again I dare not.
scene_summary: Macbeth murders Duncan, and the couple reacts to guilt and fear.

speaker: LADY MACBETH
text: Infirm of purpose! Give me the daggers. The sleeping and the dead Are but as pictures; 'tis the eye of childhood That fears a painted devil. If he do bleed, I'll gild the faces of the grooms withal, For it must seem their guilt.         Exit. Knocking within.
scene_summary: Macbeth murders Duncan, and the couple reacts to guilt and fear.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Whence is that knocking? How is't with me, when every noise appals me? What hands are here? Ha, they pluck out mine eyes! Will all great Neptune's ocean wash this blood Clean from my hand? No, this my hand will rather The multitudinous seas incarnadine, Making the green one red.
scene_summary: Macbeth murders Duncan, and the couple reacts to guilt and fear.

speaker: LADY MACBETH
text: My hands are of your color, but I shame To wear a heart so white.  I hear knocking At the south entry. Retire we to our chamber. A little water clears us of this deed. How easy is it then! Your constancy Hath left you unattended.  Hark, more knocking. Get on your nightgown, lest occasion call us And show us to be watchers. Be not lost So poorly in your thoughts.
scene_summary: Macbeth murders Duncan, and the couple reacts to guilt and fear.

speaker: MACBETH
text: To know my deed, 'twere best not know myself. Knocking within. Wake Duncan with thy knocking! I would thou couldst!
scene_summary: Macbeth murders Duncan, and the couple reacts to guilt and fear.

speaker: PORTER
text: Here's a knocking indeed! If a man were porter of Hell Gate, he should have old turning the key.  Knock, knock, knock! Who's there, i' the name of Belzebub? Here's a farmer that hanged himself on th' expectation of plenty. Come in time! Have napkins enow about you; here you'll sweat for't.  Knock, knock! Who's there, in th' other devil's name? Faith, here's an equivocator that could swear in both the scales against either scale, who committed treason enough for God's sake, yet could not equivocate to heaven. O, come in, equivocator.  Knock, knock, knock! Who's there? Faith, here's an English tailor come hither, for stealing out of a French hose. Come in, tailor; here you may roast your goose.  Knock, knock! Never at quiet! What are you? But this place is too cold for hell. I'll devil-porter it no further. I had thought to have let in some of all professions, that go the primrose way to the everlasting bonfire.  Anon, anon! I pray you, remember the porter. Opens the gate.
scene_summary: Duncan’s murder is discovered and suspicion begins to spread.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Was it so late, friend, ere you went to bed, That you do lie so late?
scene_summary: Duncan’s murder is discovered and suspicion begins to spread.

speaker: PORTER
text: Faith, sir, we were carousing till the second cock; and drink, sir, is a great provoker of three things.
scene_summary: Duncan’s murder is discovered and suspicion begins to spread.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: What three things does drink especially provoke?
scene_summary: Duncan’s murder is discovered and suspicion begins to spread.

speaker: PORTER
text: Marry, sir, nose-painting, sleep, and urine. Lechery, sir, it provokes and unprovokes: it provokes the desire, but it takes away the performance. Therefore much drink may be said to be an equivocator with lechery: it makes him, and it mars him; it sets him on, and it takes him off; it persuades him and disheartens him; makes him stand to and not stand to; in conclusion, equivocates him in a sleep, and giving him the lie, leaves him.
scene_summary: Duncan’s murder is discovered and suspicion begins to spread.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: I believe drink gave thee the lie last night.
scene_summary: Duncan’s murder is discovered and suspicion begins to spread.

speaker: PORTER
text: That it did, sir, i' the very throat on me; but requited him for his lie, and, I think, being too strong for him, though he took up my legs sometime, yet I made shift to cast him.
scene_summary: Duncan’s murder is discovered and suspicion begins to spread.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Is thy master stirring?
scene_summary: Duncan’s murder is discovered and suspicion begins to spread.

speaker: LENNOX
text: Good morrow, noble sir.
scene_summary: Duncan’s murder is discovered and suspicion begins to spread.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Good morrow, both.
scene_summary: Duncan’s murder is discovered and suspicion begins to spread.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Is the King stirring, worthy Thane?
scene_summary: Duncan’s murder is discovered and suspicion begins to spread.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Not yet.
scene_summary: Duncan’s murder is discovered and suspicion begins to spread.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: He did command me to call timely on him; I have almost slipp'd the hour.
scene_summary: Duncan’s murder is discovered and suspicion begins to spread.

speaker: MACBETH
text: I'll bring you to him.
scene_summary: Duncan’s murder is discovered and suspicion begins to spread.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: I know this is a joyful trouble to you, But yet 'tis one.
scene_summary: Duncan’s murder is discovered and suspicion begins to spread.

speaker: MACBETH
text: The labor we delight in physics pain. This is the door.
scene_summary: Duncan’s murder is discovered and suspicion begins to spread.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: I'll make so bold to call, For 'tis my limited service.                           Exit.
scene_summary: Duncan’s murder is discovered and suspicion begins to spread.

speaker: LENNOX
text: Goes the King hence today?
scene_summary: Duncan’s murder is discovered and suspicion begins to spread.

speaker: MACBETH
text: He does; he did appoint so.
scene_summary: Duncan’s murder is discovered and suspicion begins to spread.

speaker: LENNOX
text: The night has been unruly. Where we lay, Our chimneys were blown down, and, as they say, Lamentings heard i' the air, strange screams of death, And prophesying with accents terrible Of dire combustion and confused events New hatch'd to the woeful time. The obscure bird Clamor'd the livelong night. Some say the earth Was feverous and did shake.
scene_summary: Duncan’s murder is discovered and suspicion begins to spread.

speaker: MACBETH
text: 'Twas a rough night.
scene_summary: Duncan’s murder is discovered and suspicion begins to spread.

speaker: LENNOX
text: My young remembrance cannot parallel A fellow to it.
scene_summary: Duncan’s murder is discovered and suspicion begins to spread.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: O horror, horror, horror! Tongue nor heart Cannot conceive nor name thee.
scene_summary: Duncan’s murder is discovered and suspicion begins to spread.

speaker: MACBETH
text: LENNOX. What's the matter?
scene_summary: Duncan’s murder is discovered and suspicion begins to spread.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Confusion now hath made his masterpiece. Most sacrilegious murther hath broke ope The Lord's anointed temple and stole thence The life o' the building.
scene_summary: Duncan’s murder is discovered and suspicion begins to spread.

speaker: MACBETH
text: What is't you say? the life?
scene_summary: Duncan’s murder is discovered and suspicion begins to spread.

speaker: LENNOX
text: Mean you his Majesty?
scene_summary: Duncan’s murder is discovered and suspicion begins to spread.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Approach the chamber, and destroy your sight With a new Gorgon. Do not bid me speak; See, and then speak yourselves.
scene_summary: Duncan’s murder is discovered and suspicion begins to spread.

speaker: LADY MACBETH
text: What's the business, That such a hideous trumpet calls to parley The sleepers of the house? Speak, speak!
scene_summary: Duncan’s murder is discovered and suspicion begins to spread.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: O gentle lady, 'Tis not for you to hear what I can speak: The repetition in a woman's ear Would murther as it fell.
scene_summary: Duncan’s murder is discovered and suspicion begins to spread.

speaker: LADY MACBETH
text: Woe, alas! What, in our house?
scene_summary: Duncan’s murder is discovered and suspicion begins to spread.

speaker: BANQUO
text: Too cruel anywhere. Dear Duff, I prithee, contradict thyself, And say it is not so.
scene_summary: Duncan’s murder is discovered and suspicion begins to spread.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Had I but died an hour before this chance, I had lived a blessed time, for from this instant There's nothing serious in mortality. All is but toys; renown and grace is dead, The wine of life is drawn, and the mere lees Is left this vault to brag of.
scene_summary: Duncan’s murder is discovered and suspicion begins to spread.

speaker: DONALBAIN
text: What is amiss?
scene_summary: Duncan’s murder is discovered and suspicion begins to spread.

speaker: MACBETH
text: You are, and do not know't. The spring, the head, the fountain of your blood Is stopped, the very source of it is stopp'd.
scene_summary: Duncan’s murder is discovered and suspicion begins to spread.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Your royal father's murther'd.
scene_summary: Duncan’s murder is discovered and suspicion begins to spread.

speaker: MALCOLM
text: O, by whom?
scene_summary: Duncan’s murder is discovered and suspicion begins to spread.

speaker: LENNOX
text: Those of his chamber, as it seem'd, had done't. Their hands and faces were all badged with blood; So were their daggers, which unwiped we found Upon their pillows. They stared, and were distracted; no man's life Was to be trusted with them.
scene_summary: Duncan’s murder is discovered and suspicion begins to spread.

speaker: MACBETH
text: O, yet I do repent me of my fury, That I did kill them.
scene_summary: Duncan’s murder is discovered and suspicion begins to spread.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Wherefore did you so?
scene_summary: Duncan’s murder is discovered and suspicion begins to spread.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Who can be wise, amazed, temperate and furious, Loyal and neutral, in a moment? No man. The expedition of my violent love Outrun the pauser reason. Here lay Duncan, His silver skin laced with his golden blood, And his gash'd stabs look'd like a breach in nature For ruin's wasteful entrance; there, the murtherers, Steep'd in the colors of their trade, their daggers Unmannerly breech'd with gore. Who could refrain, That had a heart to love, and in that heart Courage to make 's love known?
scene_summary: Duncan’s murder is discovered and suspicion begins to spread.

speaker: LADY MACBETH
text: Help me hence, ho!
scene_summary: Duncan’s murder is discovered and suspicion begins to spread.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Look to the lady.
scene_summary: Duncan’s murder is discovered and suspicion begins to spread.

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Why do we hold our tongues, That most may claim this argument for ours?
scene_summary: Duncan’s murder is discovered and suspicion begins to spread.

speaker: DONALBAIN
text: What should be spoken here, where our fate, Hid in an auger hole, may rush and seize us? Let's away, Our tears are not yet brew'd.
scene_summary: Duncan’s murder is discovered and suspicion begins to spread.

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Nor our strong sorrow Upon the foot of motion.
scene_summary: Duncan’s murder is discovered and suspicion begins to spread.

speaker: BANQUO
text: Look to the lady. Lady Macbeth is carried out. And when we have our naked frailties hid, That suffer in exposure, let us meet And question this most bloody piece of work To know it further. Fears and scruples shake us. In the great hand of God I stand, and thence Against the undivulged pretense I fight Of treasonous malice.
scene_summary: Duncan’s murder is discovered and suspicion begins to spread.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: And so do I.
scene_summary: Duncan’s murder is discovered and suspicion begins to spread.

speaker: ALL
text: So all.
scene_summary: Duncan’s murder is discovered and suspicion begins to spread.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Let's briefly put on manly readiness And meet i' the hall together.
scene_summary: Duncan’s murder is discovered and suspicion begins to spread.

speaker: ALL
text: Well contented.
scene_summary: Duncan’s murder is discovered and suspicion begins to spread.

speaker: MALCOLM
text: What will you do? Let's not consort with them. To show an unfelt sorrow is an office Which the false man does easy. I'll to England.
scene_summary: Duncan’s murder is discovered and suspicion begins to spread.

speaker: DONALBAIN
text: To Ireland, I; our separated fortune Shall keep us both the safer. Where we are There's daggers in men's smiles; the near in blood, The nearer bloody.
scene_summary: Duncan’s murder is discovered and suspicion begins to spread.

speaker: MALCOLM
text: This murtherous shaft that's shot Hath not yet lighted, and our safest way Is to avoid the aim. Therefore to horse; And let us not be dainty of leave-taking, But shift away. There's warrant in that theft Which steals itself when there's no mercy left.
scene_summary: Duncan’s murder is discovered and suspicion begins to spread.

speaker: OLD MAN
text: Threescore and ten I can remember well, Within the volume of which time I have seen Hours dreadful and things strange, but this sore night Hath trifled former knowings.
scene_summary: Strange events signal disorder; Macbeth is named king after Duncan’s sons flee.

speaker: ROSS
text: Ah, good father, Thou seest the heavens, as troubled with man's act, Threaten his bloody stage. By the clock 'tis day, And yet dark night strangles the traveling lamp. Is't night's predominance, or the day's shame, That darkness does the face of earth entomb, When living light should kiss it?
scene_summary: Strange events signal disorder; Macbeth is named king after Duncan’s sons flee.

speaker: OLD MAN
text: 'Tis unnatural, Even like the deed that's done. On Tuesday last A falcon towering in her pride of place Was by a mousing owl hawk'd at and kill'd.
scene_summary: Strange events signal disorder; Macbeth is named king after Duncan’s sons flee.

speaker: ROSS
text: And Duncan's horses-a thing most strange and certain- Beauteous and swift, the minions of their race, Turn'd wild in nature, broke their stalls, flung out, Contending 'gainst obedience, as they would make War with mankind.
scene_summary: Strange events signal disorder; Macbeth is named king after Duncan’s sons flee.

speaker: OLD MAN
text: 'Tis said they eat each other.
scene_summary: Strange events signal disorder; Macbeth is named king after Duncan’s sons flee.

speaker: ROSS
text: They did so, to the amazement of mine eyes That look'd upon't.
scene_summary: Strange events signal disorder; Macbeth is named king after Duncan’s sons flee.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Why, see you not?
scene_summary: Strange events signal disorder; Macbeth is named king after Duncan’s sons flee.

speaker: ROSS
text: Is't known who did this more than bloody deed?
scene_summary: Strange events signal disorder; Macbeth is named king after Duncan’s sons flee.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Those that Macbeth hath slain.
scene_summary: Strange events signal disorder; Macbeth is named king after Duncan’s sons flee.

speaker: ROSS
text: Alas, the day! What good could they pretend?
scene_summary: Strange events signal disorder; Macbeth is named king after Duncan’s sons flee.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: They were suborn'd: Malcolm and Donalbain, the King's two sons, Are stol'n away and fled, which puts upon them Suspicion of the deed.
scene_summary: Strange events signal disorder; Macbeth is named king after Duncan’s sons flee.

speaker: ROSS
text: 'Gainst nature still! Thriftless ambition, that wilt ravin up Thine own life's means! Then 'tis most like The sovereignty will fall upon Macbeth.
scene_summary: Strange events signal disorder; Macbeth is named king after Duncan’s sons flee.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: He is already named, and gone to Scone To be invested.
scene_summary: Strange events signal disorder; Macbeth is named king after Duncan’s sons flee.

speaker: ROSS
text: Where is Duncan's body?
scene_summary: Strange events signal disorder; Macbeth is named king after Duncan’s sons flee.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Carried to Colmekill, The sacred storehouse of his predecessors And guardian of their bones.
scene_summary: Strange events signal disorder; Macbeth is named king after Duncan’s sons flee.

speaker: ROSS
text: Will you to Scone?
scene_summary: Strange events signal disorder; Macbeth is named king after Duncan’s sons flee.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: No, cousin, I'll to Fife.
scene_summary: Strange events signal disorder; Macbeth is named king after Duncan’s sons flee.

speaker: ROSS
text: Well, I will thither.
scene_summary: Strange events signal disorder; Macbeth is named king after Duncan’s sons flee.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Well, may you see things well done there. Adieu, Lest our old robes sit easier than our new!
scene_summary: Strange events signal disorder; Macbeth is named king after Duncan’s sons flee.

speaker: ROSS
text: Farewell, father.
scene_summary: Strange events signal disorder; Macbeth is named king after Duncan’s sons flee.

speaker: OLD MAN
text: God's benison go with you and with those That would make good of bad and friends of foes!
scene_summary: Strange events signal disorder; Macbeth is named king after Duncan’s sons flee.

text: ELECTRONIC AND MACHINE READABLE COPIES MAY BE DISTRIBUTED SO LONG AS SUCH COPIES (1) ARE FOR YOUR OR OTHERS PERSONAL USE ONLY, AND (2) ARE NOT DISTRIBUTED OR USED

text: PROHIBITED COMMERCIAL DISTRIBUTION INCLUDES BY ANY SERVICE THAT CHARGES FOR DOWNLOAD TIME OR FOR MEMBERSHIP.>>
scene_summary: Strange events signal disorder; Macbeth is named king after Duncan’s sons flee.

speaker: FORRES
text: The palace.
scene_summary: Macbeth fears Banquo and arranges to murder him and Fleance.

speaker: BANQUO
text: Thou hast it now: King, Cawdor, Glamis, all, As the weird women promised, and I fear Thou play'dst most foully for't; yet it was said It should not stand in thy posterity, But that myself should be the root and father Of many kings. If there come truth from them (As upon thee, Macbeth, their speeches shine) Why, by the verities on thee made good, May they not be my oracles as well And set me up in hope? But hush, no more. Sennet sounds. Enter Macbeth as King, Lady Macbeth as Queen, Lennox, Ross, Lords, Ladies, and Attendants.
scene_summary: Macbeth fears Banquo and arranges to murder him and Fleance.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Here's our chief guest.
scene_summary: Macbeth fears Banquo and arranges to murder him and Fleance.

speaker: LADY MACBETH
text: If he had been forgotten, It had been as a gap in our great feast And all thing unbecoming.
scene_summary: Macbeth fears Banquo and arranges to murder him and Fleance.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Tonight we hold a solemn supper, sir, And I'll request your presence.
scene_summary: Macbeth fears Banquo and arranges to murder him and Fleance.

speaker: BANQUO
text: Let your Highness Command upon me, to the which my duties Are with a most indissoluble tie Forever knit.
scene_summary: Macbeth fears Banquo and arranges to murder him and Fleance.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Ride you this afternoon?
scene_summary: Macbeth fears Banquo and arranges to murder him and Fleance.

speaker: BANQUO
text: Ay, my good lord.
scene_summary: Macbeth fears Banquo and arranges to murder him and Fleance.

speaker: MACBETH
text: We should have else desired your good advice, Which still hath been both grave and prosperous In this day's council; but we'll take tomorrow. Is't far you ride?
scene_summary: Macbeth fears Banquo and arranges to murder him and Fleance.

speaker: BANQUO
text: As far, my lord, as will fill up the time 'Twixt this and supper. Go not my horse the better, I must become a borrower of the night For a dark hour or twain.
scene_summary: Macbeth fears Banquo and arranges to murder him and Fleance.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Fail not our feast.
scene_summary: Macbeth fears Banquo and arranges to murder him and Fleance.

speaker: BANQUO
text: My lord, I will not.
scene_summary: Macbeth fears Banquo and arranges to murder him and Fleance.

speaker: MACBETH
text: We hear our bloody cousins are bestow'd In England and in Ireland, not confessing Their cruel parricide, filling their hearers With strange invention. But of that tomorrow, When therewithal we shall have cause of state Craving us jointly. Hie you to horse; adieu, Till you return at night. Goes Fleance with you?
scene_summary: Macbeth fears Banquo and arranges to murder him and Fleance.

speaker: BANQUO
text: Ay, my good lord. Our time does call upon 's.
scene_summary: Macbeth fears Banquo and arranges to murder him and Fleance.

speaker: MACBETH
text: I wish your horses swift and sure of foot, And so I do commend you to their backs.
scene_summary: Macbeth fears Banquo and arranges to murder him and Fleance.

speaker: FAREWELL
text: Exit Banquo. Let every man be master of his time Till seven at night; to make society The sweeter welcome, we will keep ourself Till supper time alone. While then, God be with you!
scene_summary: Macbeth fears Banquo and arranges to murder him and Fleance.

speaker: ATTENDANT
text: They are, my lord, without the palace gate.
scene_summary: Macbeth fears Banquo and arranges to murder him and Fleance.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Bring them before us.                 Exit Attendant. To be thus is nothing, But to be safely thus. Our fears in Banquo. Stick deep, and in his royalty of nature Reigns that which would be fear'd. 'Tis much he dares, And, to that dauntless temper of his mind, He hath a wisdom that doth guide his valor To act in safety. There is none but he Whose being I do fear; and under him My genius is rebuked, as it is said Mark Antony's was by Caesar. He chid the sisters When first they put the name of King upon me And bade them speak to him; then prophet-like They hail'd him father to a line of kings. Upon my head they placed a fruitless crown And put a barren sceptre in my gripe, Thence to be wrench'd with an unlineal hand, No son of mine succeeding. If't be so, For Banquo's issue have I filed my mind, For them the gracious Duncan have I murther'd, Put rancors in the vessel of my peace Only for them, and mine eternal jewel Given to the common enemy of man, To make them kings -the seed of Banquo kings! Rather than so, come, Fate, into the list, And champion me to the utterance! Who's there?
scene_summary: Macbeth fears Banquo and arranges to murder him and Fleance.

speaker: FIRST MURTHERER
text: It was, so please your Highness.
scene_summary: Macbeth fears Banquo and arranges to murder him and Fleance.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Well then, now Have you consider'd of my speeches? Know That it was he in the times past which held you So under fortune, which you thought had been Our innocent self? This I made good to you In our last conference, pass'd in probation with you: How you were borne in hand, how cross'd, the instruments, Who wrought with them, and all things else that might To half a soul and to a notion crazed Say, "Thus did Banquo."
scene_summary: Macbeth fears Banquo and arranges to murder him and Fleance.

speaker: FIRST MURTHERER
text: You made it known to us.
scene_summary: Macbeth fears Banquo and arranges to murder him and Fleance.

speaker: MACBETH
text: I did so, and went further, which is now Our point of second meeting. Do you find Your patience so predominant in your nature, That you can let this go? Are you so gospel'd, To pray for this good man and for his issue, Whose heavy hand hath bow'd you to the grave And beggar'd yours forever?
scene_summary: Macbeth fears Banquo and arranges to murder him and Fleance.

speaker: FIRST MURTHERER
text: We are men, my liege.
scene_summary: Macbeth fears Banquo and arranges to murder him and Fleance.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Ay, in the catalogue ye go for men, As hounds and greyhounds, mongrels, spaniels, curs, Shoughs, waterrugs, and demi-wolves are clept All by the name of dogs. The valued file Distinguishes the swift, the slow, the subtle, The housekeeper, the hunter, every one According to the gift which bounteous nature Hath in him closed, whereby he does receive Particular addition, from the bill That writes them all alike; and so of men. Now if you have a station in the file, Not i' the worst rank of manhood, say it, And I will put that business in your bosoms Whose execution takes your enemy off, Grapples you to the heart and love of us, Who wear our health but sickly in his life, Which in his death were perfect.
scene_summary: Macbeth fears Banquo and arranges to murder him and Fleance.

speaker: SECOND MURTHERER
text: I am one, my liege, Whom the vile blows and buffets of the world Have so incensed that I am reckless what I do to spite the world.
scene_summary: Macbeth fears Banquo and arranges to murder him and Fleance.

speaker: FIRST MURTHERER
text: And I another So weary with disasters, tugg'd with fortune, That I would set my life on any chance, To mend it or be rid on't.
scene_summary: Macbeth fears Banquo and arranges to murder him and Fleance.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Both of you Know Banquo was your enemy.
scene_summary: Macbeth fears Banquo and arranges to murder him and Fleance.

speaker: BOTH MURTHERERS
text: True, my lord.
scene_summary: Macbeth fears Banquo and arranges to murder him and Fleance.

speaker: MACBETH
text: So is he mine, and in such bloody distance That every minute of his being thrusts Against my near'st of life; and though I could With barefaced power sweep him from my sight And bid my will avouch it, yet I must not, For certain friends that are both his and mine, Whose loves I may not drop, but wail his fall Who I myself struck down. And thence it is That I to your assistance do make love, Masking the business from the common eye For sundry weighty reasons.
scene_summary: Macbeth fears Banquo and arranges to murder him and Fleance.

speaker: SECOND MURTHERER
text: We shall, my lord, Perform what you command us.
scene_summary: Macbeth fears Banquo and arranges to murder him and Fleance.

speaker: FIRST MURTHERER
text: Though our lives-
scene_summary: Macbeth fears Banquo and arranges to murder him and Fleance.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Your spirits shine through you. Within this hour at most I will advise you where to plant yourselves, Acquaint you with the perfect spy o' the time, The moment on't; for't must be done tonight And something from the palace (always thought That I require a clearness); and with him- To leave no rubs nor botches in the work- Fleance his son, that keeps him company, Whose absence is no less material to me Than is his father's, must embrace the fate Of that dark hour. Resolve yourselves apart; I'll come to you anon.
scene_summary: Macbeth fears Banquo and arranges to murder him and Fleance.

speaker: BOTH MURTHERERS
text: We are resolved, my lord.
scene_summary: Macbeth fears Banquo and arranges to murder him and Fleance.

speaker: MACBETH
text: I'll call upon you straight. Abide within.
scene_summary: Macbeth fears Banquo and arranges to murder him and Fleance.

speaker: LADY MACBETH
text: Is Banquo gone from court?
scene_summary: Macbeth and Lady Macbeth reflect on insecurity after gaining power.

speaker: SERVANT
text: Ay, madam, but returns again tonight.
scene_summary: Macbeth and Lady Macbeth reflect on insecurity after gaining power.

speaker: LADY MACBETH
text: Say to the King I would attend his leisure For a few words.
scene_summary: Macbeth and Lady Macbeth reflect on insecurity after gaining power.

speaker: SERVANT
text: Madam, I will.                                  Exit.
scene_summary: Macbeth and Lady Macbeth reflect on insecurity after gaining power.

speaker: LADY MACBETH
text: Nought's had, all's spent, Where our desire is got without content. 'Tis safer to be that which we destroy Than by destruction dwell in doubtful joy.
scene_summary: Macbeth and Lady Macbeth reflect on insecurity after gaining power.

speaker: MACBETH
text: We have scotch'd the snake, not kill'd it. She'll close and be herself, whilst our poor malice Remains in danger of her former tooth. But let the frame of things disjoint, both the worlds suffer, Ere we will eat our meal in fear and sleep In the affliction of these terrible dreams That shake us nightly. Better be with the dead, Whom we, to gain our peace, have sent to peace, Than on the torture of the mind to lie In restless ecstasy. Duncan is in his grave; After life's fitful fever he sleeps well. Treason has done his worst; nor steel, nor poison, Malice domestic, foreign levy, nothing, Can touch him further.
scene_summary: Macbeth and Lady Macbeth reflect on insecurity after gaining power.

speaker: LADY MACBETH
text: Come on, Gentle my lord, sleek o'er your rugged looks; Be bright and jovial among your guests tonight.
scene_summary: Macbeth and Lady Macbeth reflect on insecurity after gaining power.

speaker: MACBETH
text: So shall I, love, and so, I pray, be you. Let your remembrance apply to Banquo; Present him eminence, both with eye and tongue: Unsafe the while, that we Must lave our honors in these flattering streams, And make our faces vizards to our hearts, Disguising what they are.
scene_summary: Macbeth and Lady Macbeth reflect on insecurity after gaining power.

speaker: LADY MACBETH
text: You must leave this.
scene_summary: Macbeth and Lady Macbeth reflect on insecurity after gaining power.

speaker: MACBETH
text: O, full of scorpions is my mind, dear wife! Thou know'st that Banquo and his Fleance lives.
scene_summary: Macbeth and Lady Macbeth reflect on insecurity after gaining power.

speaker: LADY MACBETH
text: But in them nature's copy's not eterne.
scene_summary: Macbeth and Lady Macbeth reflect on insecurity after gaining power.

speaker: MACBETH
text: There's comfort yet; they are assailable. Then be thou jocund. Ere the bat hath flown His cloister'd flight, ere to black Hecate's summons The shard-borne beetle with his drowsy hums Hath rung night's yawning peal, there shall be done A deed of dreadful note.
scene_summary: Macbeth and Lady Macbeth reflect on insecurity after gaining power.

speaker: LADY MACBETH
text: What's to be done?
scene_summary: Macbeth and Lady Macbeth reflect on insecurity after gaining power.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Be innocent of the knowledge, dearest chuck, Till thou applaud the deed. Come, seeling night, Scarf up the tender eye of pitiful day, And with thy bloody and invisible hand Cancel and tear to pieces that great bond Which keeps me pale! Light thickens, and the crow Makes wing to the rooky wood; Good things of day begin to droop and drowse, Whiles night's black agents to their preys do rouse. Thou marvel'st at my words, but hold thee still: Things bad begun make strong themselves by ill. So, prithee, go with me.                             Exeunt.
scene_summary: Macbeth and Lady Macbeth reflect on insecurity after gaining power.

speaker: FIRST MURTHERER
text: But who did bid thee join with us?
scene_summary: Banquo is murdered, but Fleance escapes.

speaker: THIRD MURTHERER
text: Macbeth.
scene_summary: Banquo is murdered, but Fleance escapes.

speaker: SECOND MURTHERER
text: He needs not our mistrust, since he delivers Our offices and what we have to do To the direction just.
scene_summary: Banquo is murdered, but Fleance escapes.

speaker: FIRST MURTHERER
text: Then stand with us. The west yet glimmers with some streaks of day; Now spurs the lated traveler apace To gain the timely inn, and near approaches The subject of our watch.
scene_summary: Banquo is murdered, but Fleance escapes.

speaker: THIRD MURTHERER
text: Hark! I hear horses.
scene_summary: Banquo is murdered, but Fleance escapes.

speaker: BANQUO
text: Give us a light there, ho!
scene_summary: Banquo is murdered, but Fleance escapes.

speaker: SECOND MURTHERER
text: Then 'tis he; the rest That are within the note of expectation Already are i' the court.
scene_summary: Banquo is murdered, but Fleance escapes.

speaker: FIRST MURTHERER
text: His horses go about.
scene_summary: Banquo is murdered, but Fleance escapes.

speaker: THIRD MURTHERER
text: Almost a mile, but he does usually- So all men do -from hence to the palace gate Make it their walk.
scene_summary: Banquo is murdered, but Fleance escapes.

speaker: SECOND MURTHERER
text: A light, a light!
scene_summary: Banquo is murdered, but Fleance escapes.

speaker: THIRD MURTHERER
text: 'Tis he.
scene_summary: Banquo is murdered, but Fleance escapes.

speaker: FIRST MURTHERER
text: Stand to't.
scene_summary: Banquo is murdered, but Fleance escapes.

speaker: BANQUO
text: It will be rain tonight.
scene_summary: Banquo is murdered, but Fleance escapes.

speaker: FIRST MURTHERER
text: Let it come down. They set upon Banquo.
scene_summary: Banquo is murdered, but Fleance escapes.

speaker: BANQUO
text: O, treachery! Fly, good Fleance, fly, fly, fly! Thou mayst revenge. O slave!          Dies. Fleance escapes.
scene_summary: Banquo is murdered, but Fleance escapes.

speaker: THIRD MURTHERER
text: Who did strike out the light?
scene_summary: Banquo is murdered, but Fleance escapes.

speaker: FIRST MURTHERER
text: Wast not the way?
scene_summary: Banquo is murdered, but Fleance escapes.

speaker: THIRD MURTHERER
text: There's but one down; the son is fled.
scene_summary: Banquo is murdered, but Fleance escapes.

speaker: SECOND MURTHERER
text: We have lost Best half of our affair.
scene_summary: Banquo is murdered, but Fleance escapes.

speaker: FIRST MURTHERER
text: Well, let's away and say how much is done.
scene_summary: Banquo is murdered, but Fleance escapes.

speaker: MACBETH
text: You know your own degrees; sit down. At first And last the hearty welcome.
location: A Hall in the palace. A banquet prepared
scene_summary: Banquo’s ghost appears at Macbeth’s banquet, revealing Macbeth’s instability.

speaker: LORDS
text: Thanks to your Majesty.
location: A Hall in the palace. A banquet prepared
scene_summary: Banquo’s ghost appears at Macbeth’s banquet, revealing Macbeth’s instability.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Ourself will mingle with society And play the humble host. Our hostess keeps her state, but in best time We will require her welcome.
location: A Hall in the palace. A banquet prepared
scene_summary: Banquo’s ghost appears at Macbeth’s banquet, revealing Macbeth’s instability.

speaker: LADY MACBETH
text: Pronounce it for me, sir, to all our friends, For my heart speaks they are welcome.
location: A Hall in the palace. A banquet prepared
scene_summary: Banquo’s ghost appears at Macbeth’s banquet, revealing Macbeth’s instability.

speaker: MACBETH
text: See, they encounter thee with their hearts' thanks. Both sides are even; here I'll sit i' the midst. Be large in mirth; anon we'll drink a measure The table round.  There's blood upon thy face.
location: A Hall in the palace. A banquet prepared
scene_summary: Banquo’s ghost appears at Macbeth’s banquet, revealing Macbeth’s instability.

speaker: MURTHERER
text: 'Tis Banquo's then.
location: A Hall in the palace. A banquet prepared
scene_summary: Banquo’s ghost appears at Macbeth’s banquet, revealing Macbeth’s instability.

speaker: MACBETH
text: 'Tis better thee without than he within. Is he dispatch'd?
location: A Hall in the palace. A banquet prepared
scene_summary: Banquo’s ghost appears at Macbeth’s banquet, revealing Macbeth’s instability.

speaker: MURTHERER
text: My lord, his throat is cut; that I did for him.
location: A Hall in the palace. A banquet prepared
scene_summary: Banquo’s ghost appears at Macbeth’s banquet, revealing Macbeth’s instability.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Thou art the best o' the cut-throats! Yet he's good That did the like for Fleance. If thou didst it, Thou art the nonpareil.
location: A Hall in the palace. A banquet prepared
scene_summary: Banquo’s ghost appears at Macbeth’s banquet, revealing Macbeth’s instability.

speaker: MURTHERER
text: Most royal sir, Fleance is 'scaped.
location: A Hall in the palace. A banquet prepared
scene_summary: Banquo’s ghost appears at Macbeth’s banquet, revealing Macbeth’s instability.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Then comes my fit again. I had else been perfect, Whole as the marble, founded as the rock, As broad and general as the casing air; But now I am cabin'd, cribb'd, confin'd, bound in To saucy doubts and fears -But Banquo's safe?
location: A Hall in the palace. A banquet prepared
scene_summary: Banquo’s ghost appears at Macbeth’s banquet, revealing Macbeth’s instability.

speaker: MURTHERER
text: Ay, my good lord. Safe in a ditch he bides, With twenty trenched gashes on his head, The least a death to nature.
location: A Hall in the palace. A banquet prepared
scene_summary: Banquo’s ghost appears at Macbeth’s banquet, revealing Macbeth’s instability.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Thanks for that. There the grown serpent lies; the worm that's fled Hath nature that in time will venom breed, No teeth for the present. Get thee gone. Tomorrow We'll hear ourselves again.
location: A Hall in the palace. A banquet prepared
scene_summary: Banquo’s ghost appears at Macbeth’s banquet, revealing Macbeth’s instability.

speaker: LADY MACBETH
text: My royal lord, You do not give the cheer. The feast is sold That is not often vouch'd, while 'tis amaking, 'Tis given with welcome. To feed were best at home; From thence the sauce to meat is ceremony; Meeting were bare without it.
location: A Hall in the palace. A banquet prepared
scene_summary: Banquo’s ghost appears at Macbeth’s banquet, revealing Macbeth’s instability.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Sweet remembrancer! Now good digestion wait on appetite, And health on both!
location: A Hall in the palace. A banquet prepared
scene_summary: Banquo’s ghost appears at Macbeth’s banquet, revealing Macbeth’s instability.

speaker: LENNOX
text: May't please your Highness sit. The Ghost of Banquo enters and sits in Macbeth's place.
location: A Hall in the palace. A banquet prepared
scene_summary: Banquo’s ghost appears at Macbeth’s banquet, revealing Macbeth’s instability.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Here had we now our country's honor roof'd, Were the graced person of our Banquo present, Who may I rather challenge for unkindness Than pity for mischance!
location: A Hall in the palace. A banquet prepared
scene_summary: Banquo’s ghost appears at Macbeth’s banquet, revealing Macbeth’s instability.

speaker: ROSS
text: His absence, sir, Lays blame upon his promise. Please't your Highness To grace us with your royal company?
location: A Hall in the palace. A banquet prepared
scene_summary: Banquo’s ghost appears at Macbeth’s banquet, revealing Macbeth’s instability.

speaker: MACBETH
text: The table's full.
location: A Hall in the palace. A banquet prepared
scene_summary: Banquo’s ghost appears at Macbeth’s banquet, revealing Macbeth’s instability.

speaker: LENNOX
text: Here is a place reserved, sir.
location: A Hall in the palace. A banquet prepared
scene_summary: Banquo’s ghost appears at Macbeth’s banquet, revealing Macbeth’s instability.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Where?
location: A Hall in the palace. A banquet prepared
scene_summary: Banquo’s ghost appears at Macbeth’s banquet, revealing Macbeth’s instability.

speaker: LENNOX
text: Here, my good lord. What is't that moves your Highness?
location: A Hall in the palace. A banquet prepared
scene_summary: Banquo’s ghost appears at Macbeth’s banquet, revealing Macbeth’s instability.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Which of you have done this?
location: A Hall in the palace. A banquet prepared
scene_summary: Banquo’s ghost appears at Macbeth’s banquet, revealing Macbeth’s instability.

speaker: LORDS
text: What, my good lord?
location: A Hall in the palace. A banquet prepared
scene_summary: Banquo’s ghost appears at Macbeth’s banquet, revealing Macbeth’s instability.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Thou canst not say I did it; never shake Thy gory locks at me.
location: A Hall in the palace. A banquet prepared
scene_summary: Banquo’s ghost appears at Macbeth’s banquet, revealing Macbeth’s instability.

speaker: ROSS
text: Gentlemen, rise; his Highness is not well.
location: A Hall in the palace. A banquet prepared
scene_summary: Banquo’s ghost appears at Macbeth’s banquet, revealing Macbeth’s instability.

speaker: LADY MACBETH
text: Sit, worthy friends; my lord is often thus, And hath been from his youth. Pray you, keep seat. The fit is momentary; upon a thought He will again be well. If much you note him, You shall offend him and extend his passion. Feed, and regard him not-Are you a man?
location: A Hall in the palace. A banquet prepared
scene_summary: Banquo’s ghost appears at Macbeth’s banquet, revealing Macbeth’s instability.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Ay, and a bold one, that dare look on that Which might appal the devil.
location: A Hall in the palace. A banquet prepared
scene_summary: Banquo’s ghost appears at Macbeth’s banquet, revealing Macbeth’s instability.

speaker: LADY MACBETH
text: O proper stuff! This is the very painting of your fear; This is the air-drawn dagger which you said Led you to Duncan. O, these flaws and starts, Impostors to true fear, would well become A woman's story at a winter's fire, Authorized by her grandam. Shame itself! Why do you make such faces? When all's done, You look but on a stool.
location: A Hall in the palace. A banquet prepared
scene_summary: Banquo’s ghost appears at Macbeth’s banquet, revealing Macbeth’s instability.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Prithee, see there! Behold! Look! Lo! How say you? Why, what care I? If thou canst nod, speak too. If charnel houses and our graves must send Those that we bury back, our monuments Shall be the maws of kites.                      Exit Ghost.
location: A Hall in the palace. A banquet prepared
scene_summary: Banquo’s ghost appears at Macbeth’s banquet, revealing Macbeth’s instability.

speaker: LADY MACBETH
text: What, quite unmann'd in folly?
location: A Hall in the palace. A banquet prepared
scene_summary: Banquo’s ghost appears at Macbeth’s banquet, revealing Macbeth’s instability.

speaker: MACBETH
text: If I stand here, I saw him.
location: A Hall in the palace. A banquet prepared
scene_summary: Banquo’s ghost appears at Macbeth’s banquet, revealing Macbeth’s instability.

speaker: LADY MACBETH
text: Fie, for shame!
location: A Hall in the palace. A banquet prepared
scene_summary: Banquo’s ghost appears at Macbeth’s banquet, revealing Macbeth’s instability.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Blood hath been shed ere now, i' the olden time, Ere humane statute purged the gentle weal; Ay, and since too, murthers have been perform'd Too terrible for the ear. The time has been, That, when the brains were out, the man would die, And there an end; but now they rise again, With twenty mortal murthers on their crowns, And push us from our stools. This is more strange Than such a murther is.
location: A Hall in the palace. A banquet prepared
scene_summary: Banquo’s ghost appears at Macbeth’s banquet, revealing Macbeth’s instability.

speaker: LADY MACBETH
text: My worthy lord, Your noble friends do lack you.
location: A Hall in the palace. A banquet prepared
scene_summary: Banquo’s ghost appears at Macbeth’s banquet, revealing Macbeth’s instability.

speaker: MACBETH
text: I do forget. Do not muse at me, my most worthy friends. I have a strange infirmity, which is nothing To those that know me. Come, love and health to all; Then I'll sit down. Give me some wine, fill full. I drink to the general joy o' the whole table, And to our dear friend Banquo, whom we miss. Would he were here! To all and him we thirst, And all to all.
location: A Hall in the palace. A banquet prepared
scene_summary: Banquo’s ghost appears at Macbeth’s banquet, revealing Macbeth’s instability.

speaker: LORDS
text: Our duties and the pledge.
location: A Hall in the palace. A banquet prepared
scene_summary: Banquo’s ghost appears at Macbeth’s banquet, revealing Macbeth’s instability.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Avaunt, and quit my sight! Let the earth hide thee! Thy bones are marrowless, thy blood is cold; Thou hast no speculation in those eyes Which thou dost glare with.
location: A Hall in the palace. A banquet prepared
scene_summary: Banquo’s ghost appears at Macbeth’s banquet, revealing Macbeth’s instability.

speaker: LADY MACBETH
text: Think of this, good peers, But as a thing of custom. 'Tis no other, Only it spoils the pleasure of the time.
location: A Hall in the palace. A banquet prepared
scene_summary: Banquo’s ghost appears at Macbeth’s banquet, revealing Macbeth’s instability.

speaker: MACBETH
text: What man dare, I dare. Approach thou like the rugged Russian bear, The arm'd rhinoceros, or the Hyrcan tiger; Take any shape but that, and my firm nerves Shall never tremble. Or be alive again, And dare me to the desert with thy sword. If trembling I inhabit then, protest me The baby of a girl. Hence, horrible shadow! Unreal mockery, hence!                           Exit Ghost. Why, so, being gone, I am a man again. Pray you sit still.
location: A Hall in the palace. A banquet prepared
scene_summary: Banquo’s ghost appears at Macbeth’s banquet, revealing Macbeth’s instability.

speaker: LADY MACBETH
text: You have displaced the mirth, broke the good meeting, With most admired disorder.
location: A Hall in the palace. A banquet prepared
scene_summary: Banquo’s ghost appears at Macbeth’s banquet, revealing Macbeth’s instability.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Can such things be, And overcome us like a summer's cloud, Without our special wonder? You make me strange Even to the disposition that I owe When now I think you can behold such sights And keep the natural ruby of your cheeks When mine is blanch'd with fear.
location: A Hall in the palace. A banquet prepared
scene_summary: Banquo’s ghost appears at Macbeth’s banquet, revealing Macbeth’s instability.

speaker: ROSS
text: What sights, my lord?
location: A Hall in the palace. A banquet prepared
scene_summary: Banquo’s ghost appears at Macbeth’s banquet, revealing Macbeth’s instability.

speaker: LADY MACBETH
text: I pray you, speak not; he grows worse and worse; Question enrages him. At once, good night. Stand not upon the order of your going, But go at once.
location: A Hall in the palace. A banquet prepared
scene_summary: Banquo’s ghost appears at Macbeth’s banquet, revealing Macbeth’s instability.

speaker: LENNOX
text: Good night, and better health Attend his Majesty!
location: A Hall in the palace. A banquet prepared
scene_summary: Banquo’s ghost appears at Macbeth’s banquet, revealing Macbeth’s instability.

speaker: LADY MACBETH
text: A kind good night to all!
location: A Hall in the palace. A banquet prepared
scene_summary: Banquo’s ghost appears at Macbeth’s banquet, revealing Macbeth’s instability.

speaker: MACBETH
text: It will have blood; they say blood will have blood. Stones have been known to move and trees to speak; Augures and understood relations have By maggot pies and choughs and rooks brought forth The secret'st man of blood. What is the night?
location: A Hall in the palace. A banquet prepared
scene_summary: Banquo’s ghost appears at Macbeth’s banquet, revealing Macbeth’s instability.

speaker: LADY MACBETH
text: Almost at odds with morning, which is which.
location: A Hall in the palace. A banquet prepared
scene_summary: Banquo’s ghost appears at Macbeth’s banquet, revealing Macbeth’s instability.

speaker: MACBETH
text: How say'st thou, that Macduff denies his person At our great bidding?
location: A Hall in the palace. A banquet prepared
scene_summary: Banquo’s ghost appears at Macbeth’s banquet, revealing Macbeth’s instability.

speaker: LADY MACBETH
text: Did you send to him, sir?
location: A Hall in the palace. A banquet prepared
scene_summary: Banquo’s ghost appears at Macbeth’s banquet, revealing Macbeth’s instability.

speaker: MACBETH
text: I hear it by the way, but I will send. There's not a one of them but in his house I keep a servant feed. I will tomorrow, And betimes I will, to the weird sisters. More shall they speak; for now I am bent to know, By the worst means, the worst. For mine own good All causes shall give way. I am in blood Stepp'd in so far that, should I wade no more, Returning were as tedious as go o'er. Strange things I have in head that will to hand, Which must be acted ere they may be scann'd.
location: A Hall in the palace. A banquet prepared
scene_summary: Banquo’s ghost appears at Macbeth’s banquet, revealing Macbeth’s instability.

speaker: LADY MACBETH
text: You lack the season of all natures, sleep.
location: A Hall in the palace. A banquet prepared
scene_summary: Banquo’s ghost appears at Macbeth’s banquet, revealing Macbeth’s instability.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Come, we'll to sleep. My strange and self-abuse Is the initiate fear that wants hard use. We are yet but young in deed.                       Exeunt.
location: A Hall in the palace. A banquet prepared
scene_summary: Banquo’s ghost appears at Macbeth’s banquet, revealing Macbeth’s instability.

speaker: FIRST WITCH
text: Why, how now, Hecate? You look angerly.
scene_summary: Hecate prepares to mislead Macbeth further.

speaker: HECATE
text: Have I not reason, beldams as you are, Saucy and overbold? How did you dare To trade and traffic with Macbeth In riddles and affairs of death, And I, the mistress of your charms, The close contriver of all harms, Was never call'd to bear my part, Or show the glory of our art? And, which is worse, all you have done Hath been but for a wayward son, Spiteful and wrathful, who, as others do, Loves for his own ends, not for you. But make amends now. Get you gone, And at the pit of Acheron Meet me i' the morning. Thither he Will come to know his destiny. Your vessels and your spells provide, Your charms and everything beside. I am for the air; this night I'll spend Unto a dismal and a fatal end. Great business must be wrought ere noon: Upon the corner of the moon There hangs a vaporous drop profound; I'll catch it ere it come to ground. And that distill'd by magic sleights Shall raise such artificial sprites As by the strength of their illusion Shall draw him on to his confusion. He shall spurn fate, scorn death, and bear His hopes 'bove wisdom, grace, and fear. And you all know security Is mortals' chiefest enemy. Music and a song within, "Come away, come away." Hark! I am call'd; my little spirit, see, Sits in a foggy cloud and stays for me.                Exit.
scene_summary: Hecate prepares to mislead Macbeth further.

speaker: FIRST WITCH
text: Come, let's make haste; she'll soon be back again.
scene_summary: Hecate prepares to mislead Macbeth further.

speaker: FORRES
text: The palace.
scene_summary: Scottish nobles discuss Macbeth’s tyranny and hope for resistance.

speaker: LENNOX
text: My former speeches have but hit your thoughts, Which can interpret farther; only I say Thing's have been strangely borne. The gracious Duncan Was pitied of Macbeth; marry, he was dead. And the right valiant Banquo walk'd too late, Whom, you may say, if't please you, Fleance kill'd, For Fleance fled. Men must not walk too late. Who cannot want the thought, how monstrous It was for Malcolm and for Donalbain To kill their gracious father? Damned fact! How it did grieve Macbeth! Did he not straight, In pious rage, the two delinquents tear That were the slaves of drink and thralls of sleep? Was not that nobly done? Ay, and wisely too, For 'twould have anger'd any heart alive To hear the men deny't. So that, I say, He has borne all things well; and I do think That, had he Duncan's sons under his key- As, an't please heaven, he shall not -they should find What 'twere to kill a father; so should Fleance. But, peace! For from broad words, and 'cause he fail'd His presence at the tyrant's feast, I hear, Macduff lives in disgrace. Sir, can you tell Where he bestows himself?
scene_summary: Scottish nobles discuss Macbeth’s tyranny and hope for resistance.

speaker: LORD
text: The son of Duncan, From whom this tyrant holds the due of birth, Lives in the English court and is received Of the most pious Edward with such grace That the malevolence of fortune nothing Takes from his high respect. Thither Macduff Is gone to pray the holy King, upon his aid To wake Northumberland and warlike Siward; That by the help of these, with Him above To ratify the work, we may again Give to our tables meat, sleep to our nights, Free from our feasts and banquets bloody knives, Do faithful homage, and receive free honors- All which we pine for now. And this report Hath so exasperate the King that he Prepares for some attempt of war.
scene_summary: Scottish nobles discuss Macbeth’s tyranny and hope for resistance.

speaker: LENNOX
text: Sent he to Macduff?
scene_summary: Scottish nobles discuss Macbeth’s tyranny and hope for resistance.

speaker: LORD
text: He did, and with an absolute "Sir, not I," The cloudy messenger turns me his back, And hums, as who should say, "You'll rue the time That clogs me with this answer."
scene_summary: Scottish nobles discuss Macbeth’s tyranny and hope for resistance.

speaker: LENNOX
text: And that well might Advise him to a caution, to hold what distance His wisdom can provide. Some holy angel Fly to the court of England and unfold His message ere he come, that a swift blessing May soon return to this our suffering country Under a hand accursed!
scene_summary: Scottish nobles discuss Macbeth’s tyranny and hope for resistance.

speaker: LORD
text: I'll send my prayers with him.
scene_summary: Scottish nobles discuss Macbeth’s tyranny and hope for resistance.
